title: carex rigida subsp. decolorans
intro: Carex rigida subsp. decolorans, also known as the rigid sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The rigid sedge has been used to reduce fever, alleviate cold symptoms, and improve respiratory health, offering numerous health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
